codeReview.summary: Clean, minimal 96-line implementation that covers all 8 expected behaviors. Subclasses SimpleHTTPRequestHandler with a WriteHandler that uses regex-based route matching for two POST endpoints. JSON validation, auto-directory creation, and error responses are all correctly implemented. The regex character class [A-Za-z0-9_-] appropriately restricts the week parameter to prevent path traversal. Static file serving is correctly configured via functools.partial with directory=SITE_DIR. No blocking issues found.

issues.description: Content-Length defaults to 0 when header is missing. A POST with no Content-Length reads 0 bytes, json.loads(b"") raises JSONDecodeError → returns 400. This is acceptable behavior for this use case but could be made more explicit with a dedicated check and clearer error message (e.g., 'Missing request body').

self.path includes query string, so a request to /api/labels/2025-W42?ts=123 would NOT match the regex (due to $ anchor before query params) and would return 404. Acceptable for this single-user validation tool where no client sends query params, but worth noting if the API surface expands.

log_message override suppresses all GET request logs. This keeps output clean but could make debugging static file serving issues harder. Consider logging at a reduced level rather than fully suppressing.

summary: PASS. site/serve.py is a well-structured 96-line Python HTTP server that fulfills all 8 expected behaviors: static serving from site/ on port 8200, POST /api/labels/{week} and /api/lock-records/{week} writing JSON to disk with auto-created directories, JSON validation returning 400 on invalid input, 200 with {status: ok} on success, overwrite on repeated POST, and --port arg. The regex route matching is secure against path traversal. Three non-blocking observations noted (Content-Length edge case, query string in path, suppressed GET logs) — none require action for this use case. Worker followed skill procedure correctly, services.yaml is properly configured, and AGENTS.md conventions were respected.
